nonBlockingIssues.description: Session recovery only covers completed uploads (saved in sessionStorage after completion), not mid-upload interruptions. Feature requirement 'Session recovery if upload interrupted' is only partially addressed.
AudioRecorder lacks network-specific error messages and retry button for upload failures. Users must click 'Record Again' (discards recording) instead of retrying just the failed upload.
Dead 'discover.sessionRecovery' translation keys defined in en.json/id.json but never used by any component.
analysisId mentioned in feature spec is absent from response schema — ID generated downstream by save endpoint. Functional but deviates from literal spec.
artifactUrl uses z.string().min(1) without z.url() validation. Mitigated by XSS pattern checks, but z.url() would be stronger first-line validation.
Image mock response rotation uses Date.now() % 2, making it non-deterministic across test runs.

XSS sanitization is conditional (only when containsSuspiciousPatterns() returns true). Per architecture invariant #7, ALL user-provided text should be sanitized unconditionally before inclusion in AI prompts.
Claude client file named claude.ts instead of anthropic.ts as specified in architecture.md project structure.
Audio story submission sends hardcoded placeholder string '[Audio story recording - {filename}]' as storyText. Acceptable for mock MVP but needs real audio transcription for production.
XSS test assertion uses loose expect([200, 400]).toContain(res.status) instead of asserting definitive sanitized behavior.
Export/Save-as-Image feature mentioned in feature description is not implemented. Translation keys exist but no export button is rendered. Share functionality works.
Deep-linkable results page requires authentication, preventing unauthenticated parents from viewing shared results. Tension with 'deep-linkable for showing parents' requirement.
Promise chain in handleStoryAnalysisComplete uses .then() without .catch(), risking unhandled promise rejections.
Unused translation key 'discover.results.reasoning' defined in both en.json and id.json.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: XSS sanitization on user-provided text must be unconditional before inclusion in AI prompts. Do not gate sanitization behind containsSuspiciousPatterns() checks — always call sanitizeInput() on all user text.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature discovery-story-prompting conditionally sanitizes story text (only when containsSuspiciousPatterns returns true). Architecture invariant #7 says 'User-provided text is sanitized before inclusion in AI prompts' implying unconditional. The artifact analysis route has the same conditional pattern.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify Claude client file naming convention: document whether the canonical path is src/lib/ai/claude.ts or src/lib/ai/anthropic.ts. The architecture.md says anthropic.ts but the implementation uses claude.ts. Future workers (quest generation) need a clear reference.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: discovery-story-prompting created src/lib/ai/claude.ts. Architecture.md section 6 specifies src/lib/ai/anthropic.ts. No existing anthropic.ts file exists. Quest generation feature will also use Claude and needs consistent naming.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the deep-linking vs authentication tension: public sharing of results (e.g., showing parents) requires either a separate public route with share tokens or a guest access pattern, since all current routes require session authentication.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: discovery-results-and-history requires auth for GET /api/discovery/[id], but feature spec says 'deep-linkable for showing parents'. This tension will recur in Gallery (public browsing) and Quest completion sharing.

rejectedObservations.observation: Audio size limit discrepancy between architecture.md (50 MB) and implementation (5 MB)
rejectedObservations.reason: This is an architecture.md documentation inaccuracy, not a code bug. The 5 MB implementation matches the feature description and is the correct value. Architecture.md is a reference document maintained separately — flagging for the orchestrator would create noise. The correct value (5 MB) is self-documented in the code.

rejectedObservations.observation: EXIF stripping done server-side in storage layer, architecture.md says client-side
rejectedObservations.reason: Server-side EXIF stripping is actually more secure (cannot be bypassed by client manipulation). The architecture.md statement is outdated. Updating it is documentation maintenance, not a convention the orchestrator needs to enforce.

rejectedObservations.observation: Discover page not under (auth) route group per architecture.md structure
rejectedObservations.reason: Auth is still enforced via middleware (proxy.ts). The route group is a file organization convention, not a security boundary. The actual auth protection is intact.

rejectedObservations.observation: console.error as de facto logging convention not documented in AGENTS.md
rejectedObservations.reason: console.error is the standard Node.js/Next.js error logging approach. It doesn't need explicit documentation in AGENTS.md — it's the default behavior and all routes use it consistently.
The existing pattern is intuitive and consistent (feature.subfeature.key). AGENTS.md already says 'Use nested keys' which is sufficient guidance. Over-documenting obvious patterns adds noise.

rejectedObservations.observation: Talent category field missing from TalentSchema — name serves as category
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a downstream concern for the Gallery milestone. The discovery milestone correctly implements talent detection with name/confidence/reasoning. The gallery feature worker will need to map talent names to categories, which is a gallery-level design decision.
Dynamic imports for optional dependencies are a standard JavaScript pattern. The existing openai SDK uses the same approach. This doesn't need separate library documentation.

rejectedObservations.observation: API directory naming inconsistency (discover/ vs discovery/)
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a pre-existing architecture.md documentation error, not introduced by this milestone's features. The actual code uses 'discovery/' consistently.
